# Migration Guide: v1.4 to v1.5

Memories v1.5.0 adds multi-auth with prefix-scoped API keys. **There are no breaking changes.** Your existing setup continues to work without modification. This guide covers what changed and how to take advantage of the new capabilities.

## Summary

| Aspect | Impact |
|--------|--------|
| Breaking changes | None |
| Required action | None |
| New env vars | None |
| Data migration | None |
| Downtime | Standard container restart only |

## What Stays the Same

- **Your `API_KEY` env var** continues to work as before. It is now recognized as an "implicit admin" key with full access to everything.
- **All existing API endpoints** behave identically. Search, add, delete, extract, backup — nothing changes.
- **MCP server configuration** requires no updates. Your existing MCP clients keep working with the same key.
- **Web UI** works the same. If you are using the admin key, you will see a new "API Keys" page in the sidebar, but all other pages are unchanged.
- **No-auth mode** still works. If you do not set `API_KEY`, the server runs without authentication (suitable for local-only use).

## What Is New

### 1. Multiple API Keys

You can now create additional API keys with specific roles and prefix scopes. This is entirely opt-in — you do not need to create any managed keys if you do not want to.

### 2. Three Role Tiers

| Role | What it can do |
|------|---------------|
| `read-only` | Search and list memories (within its prefix scope) |
| `read-write` | Search, add, delete, and extract memories (within its prefix scope) |
| `admin` | Everything, plus create/manage other API keys. No prefix restrictions. |

Your existing `API_KEY` is equivalent to `admin`.

### 3. Prefix Scoping

Non-admin keys are bound to one or more source prefixes. A key with prefix `claude-code/*` can only see and modify memories whose source starts with `claude-code/`. This is enforced on all API operations.

### 4. Key Management API

Five new endpoints under `/api/keys`:

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /api/keys/me` | Check your current key's role and scope |
| `POST /api/keys` | Create a new key (admin only) |
| `GET /api/keys` | List all keys (admin only) |
| `PATCH /api/keys/{id}` | Update a key (admin only) |
| `DELETE /api/keys/{id}` | Revoke a key (admin only) |

### 5. Web UI: API Keys Page

A new page in the sidebar lets you manage keys visually. It is only visible when authenticated with an admin key.

## Upgrade Steps

### 1. Pull the Latest Image and Restart

```bash
cd /path/to/memories
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d --build
```

That is all that is required. The SQLite table for managed keys is created automatically on first startup.

### 2. Verify Your Admin Access (Optional)

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8900/api/keys/me \
  -H "X-API-Key: $API_KEY" | python3 -m json.tool
```

**Expected output:**

```json
{
    "type": "env",
    "role": "admin"
}
```

### 3. Start Creating Scoped Keys (Optional)

If you want to give agents or team members limited access, create scoped keys:

```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8900/api/keys \
  -H "X-API-Key: $API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "claude-code",
    "role": "read-write",
    "prefixes": ["claude-code/*"]
  }' | python3 -m json.tool
```

See the [Setting Up Multi-Auth tutorial](../tutorials/tut-001-setup-multi-auth.md) for a full walkthrough.

## Behavioral Differences to Be Aware Of

### New Error Codes

If you create scoped keys and use them, you may encounter two new error responses:

| Status | When |
|--------|------|
| 403 `"Source prefix not allowed"` | A scoped key tries to add/delete a memory outside its allowed prefixes |
| 403 `"Insufficient permissions"` | A read-only key tries to add, delete, or extract |

These errors only apply to managed keys with restricted roles or prefixes. Your admin key never receives these errors.

### Search Result Filtering

When using a scoped key, search results are filtered to only include memories within the key's allowed prefixes. This filtering happens server-side and is transparent to the caller — you simply get fewer results if some memories are outside your scope.

### Key Management Endpoint Protection

The `POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/keys` endpoints return 403 for non-admin keys. `GET /api/keys/me` is available to all authenticated keys.

## Rollback

If you need to roll back to v1.4, simply restart with the v1.4 image. The managed keys SQLite table will remain in your data directory but will be ignored by v1.4. No data is lost or corrupted. When you upgrade to v1.5 again, your managed keys will be available.

## FAQ

**Q: Do I have to create scoped keys?**
No. Multi-auth is entirely opt-in. Your existing `API_KEY` continues to work exactly as before.

**Q: Can I have multiple admin keys?**
Yes. You can create managed keys with the `admin` role. They have the same access as your env-based key, including the ability to manage other keys.

**Q: What happens to existing memories?**
Nothing. Memories are not modified. Prefix scoping only affects which memories a given key can see and modify — it does not change the memories themselves.

**Q: Do I need to change my MCP server configuration?**
Only if you want to use a scoped key instead of your admin key. If you are happy with your current setup, no changes are needed.

**Q: Is there a performance impact?**
Negligible. Key lookup is a single SHA-256 hash comparison followed by an SQLite query (if not the env key). Prefix filtering on search results adds minimal overhead.
